item.title: "Research"
item.description: Hi, this is my actual first post, although the title might say "my second post".

item.title: About me
item.description:
CV / Github / Google Scholar / Orcid


I am an Assistant Professor in the Department of Statistics at the University of California, Santa Cruz. Before my current position, I was a post-doctoral researcher (advised by Jacob Bien), with a Ph.D. from Carnegie Mellon University (advised by Max G'Sell and Ryan Tibshirani) and a B.S. from the University of Michigan.

I develop statistical tools for large complex data in real-world scientific applications.

My current research centers around statistically analyzing the ocean's microbial ecology using high-resolution biological and remote sensing datasets (e.g., flow cytometry, metagenomics, DNA metabarcoding, and spatial proteomics). My research often entails looking at very small things – like single-cell resolution flow cytometry data – to answer very broad questions about ocean ecology, like:

item.title: Group
item.description:
I am recruiting for highly motivated postdoctoral scholars, graduate students, and undergraduate students to join my group!

Post-docs

Riddhiman Bhattacharya

Trisha Dawn (Incoming)

Students

Current

Ethan Pawl (PhD, Jointly with Paul Parker)

Ziyue Zheng (PhD)

Dane Pearson (Undergrad)

Alumni

Rafael Catoia (MS, next position: Biostatistician, QSU Stanford Medicine)

item.title: Research
item.description:
Oceanography and biology

Fine tuning of TCR signaling via CD8αβ and PD-1 and the fate of autoreactive thymocytes during negative selection Martha C Zúñiga, Sangwon Hyun et al. Immunohorizons (2026)

Defining 3-dimensional marine provinces with phytoplankton compositions Rafael Catoia Pulgrossi, Nathan L R Williams, Yubin Raut, Jed Fuhrman, Sangwon Hyun Submitted (2025)

Mixtures of Neural Network Experts with Application to Phytoplankton Flow Cytometry Data Ethan Pawl, François Ribalet, Paul A Parker, Sangwon Hyun Submitted (2025)
